rounds.prompt: How many times faster is a modern smartphone processor than the Apollo 11 computer?
rounds.fact: The Apollo computer ran at 0.043 MHz; a modern phone chip runs at ~3,200 MHz — about 120 million× faster. Apollo 11 landed on the Moon with less power than a calculator.

rounds.prompt: How many times more transistors does a modern CPU have than the first Intel 4004?
rounds.fact: The Intel 4004 had 2,300 transistors; a modern CPU has ~100 billion — about 43 billion× more, each one smaller than a virus.

rounds.prompt: How many times larger is the internet today than in 2000 (by data stored)?
rounds.fact: In 2000, the internet stored ~1 exabyte; today it stores ~100+ zettabytes — roughly 100,000× more data in 24 years.

rounds.prompt: How many times more energy does a nuclear power plant produce than a single wind turbine?
rounds.fact: A wind turbine produces ~2 MW; a nuclear plant produces ~1,000–3,000 MW — roughly 1,500× more from a single reactor.

rounds.prompt: How many times heavier is a space shuttle than a Formula 1 car?
rounds.fact: An F1 car weighs ~798 kg; the Space Shuttle at launch weighed ~2,030,000 kg — about 2,544× heavier including fuel and boosters.
